paper_title: Monitoring population dynamics of the citrus pock caterpillar (Prays endocarpa) by sex pheromone traps in the Mekong Delta of Vietnam

entities.description: The study of insects that affect agriculture, specifically pest management in pomelo crops.
Primary author and correspondence from Can Tho University.

entities.id: College of Agriculture and Applied Biology, Can Tho University, Vietnam
entities.description: Educational and research institution in Vietnam where the authors are affiliated.
The variation in the population size and structure of the citrus pock caterpillar over time.
The process and extent of CPC larvae damaging pomelo fruit peels.
Monitoring tool using synthetic pheromones to capture male CPC moths.
The specific synthetic chemical compound used as a sex pheromone lure.
Manual inspection of pomelo orchards to record fruit damage levels.
Software used for statistical analysis of experimental data.
Positive correlation value between trapped males and fruit infestation ratios.
Mathematical model for estimating action thresholds based on trap catches.
Statistical significance level of the correlation.

entities.id: Average captured males in dry season (20.7 males/trap/2 weeks)
entities.description: High capture rate observed from November to May.
Higher infestation rate observed during the dry season (Nov to Apr).
Digital records of trapped moths and infected fruit counts across six orchards in Vinh Long province.
